name: Upper Skagit Indian Tribe v. Lundgren
question: Can a court’s exercise of in rem jurisdiction overcome an assertion of tribal sovereign immunity when the tribe has not waived immunity and Congress has not unequivocally abrogated it?
description: A case in which the Court held that its prior decision in County of Yakima v. Confederated Tribes and Bands of Yakima Nation addressed only a question of statutory interpretation, not the question whether Indian tribes have sovereign immunity in in rem lawsuits.
